texts.orig: High CO2 stripping rate and high regeneration efficiency obtained
texts.text: High CO2 stripping rate and high regeneration efficiency obtained

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  CO2 stripping rate: 4.1 kg/m 2 /h , 10 times higher than the absorption rate. Thus, only 10% membrane area is required for regeneration.
texts.text:  CO2 stripping rate: 4.1 kg/m 2 /h , 10 times higher than the absorption rate. Thus, only 10% membrane area is required for regeneration.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  CO2 purity: 97% (target is 95%), 3% is water vapor, can be further condensed.
texts.text:  CO2 purity: 97% (target is 95%), 3% is water vapor, can be further condensed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Regeneration efficiency: 66% in one stage , and can be further increased by increasing operation temperature and optimizing process design.

table_cells.text: Can achieve >90% CO 2 removal and high CO 2 purity in one stage?
Yes. Feed compression or permeate vacuum required
Determined by the dense 'skin layer', typically α = 50
No. Limited by pressure ratio, multi-step process required*
No. liquid side partial pressure of CO 2 close to zero

table_cells.text: Plans for future testing in BP2
Further membrane development based on regeneration testing results so far.
Modes III and IV are currently down selected for further study. Operation conditions will be further optimized to down select one mode for Phase III.
After regeneration mode is down selected, use of reflux in membrane desorber to improve regeneration efficiency (target: 'lean' solvent lean enough for membrane absorber).
Refinement of the process economics based on the lab test data .
Finalize testing plan for Phase III.

grid.text: Plans for future testing in BP2

Modes III and IV are currently down selected for further study. Operation conditions will be further optimized to down select one mode for Phase III.

Refinement of the process economics based on the lab test data .
Finalize testing plan for Phase III.
